## Issue / context

<!-- Link the issue this closes, or paste the design/discussion link. -->
Closes #

## Type of change

<!-- Check one. Must match the Conventional Commits type used in commits. -->
- [ ] `feat` — user-visible new capability
- [ ] `fix` — user-visible bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — internal change, no behavior change
- [ ] `perf` — performance improvement
- [ ] `test` — tests only
- [ ] `build` / `ci` — build system or CI configuration
- [ ] `chore` — maintenance, no user impact

## Test plan

<!-- How did you verify this works? Commands, screenshots, expected vs
actual output. CI green is necessary but not sufficient. -->
-
-

## Checklist

- [ ] Commit messages follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/).
- [ ] CI is green: lint, test, and security scan.
- [ ] Docs updated in the same PR (if behavior changed).
- [ ] Tests added or updated in the same PR (if logic changed).
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, or `.env` files committed.
- [ ] PR is a single logical change — split into stacked PRs if larger.
